items.title: Two Pacific Islander farm workers dead, one critical after Manjimup crash
items.summary: The men died in a vehicle crash near Manjimup in the state's South West.

items.title: Albury Hospital bed shortage hits hard ahead of Farrer by-election
items.summary: A major hospital is facing a significant bed shortage in the electorate of Farrer, where residents will soon vote in a by-election.

items.title: AFP warn of arrests on arrival for ISIS-linked families after Australia flights booked
items.summary: Flights have been booked for families linked to Islamic State fighters returning from Syria to Australia, with women in the group facing arrest and investigation after they arrive.

items.title: Iran's mosquito fleet a 'weapon of mass disruption' in Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Tasmania's 30th governor announced as Caroline Wells, chair of Royal Flying Doctor Service Tasmania
items.summary: Caroline Wells will become the 30th Governor of Tasmania after receiving formal approval from King Charles III, replacing Barbara Baker AC.

items.title: What age should a child start swimming lessons?
items.summary: Swimming ability is declining in Australia, and experts say knowing the right age to enrol your children in lessons — as well as what to do if you can't access them — is key to improving water safety.

items.title: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni issues warning amid circulation of deepfake photos
items.summary: Giorgia Meloni shares an AI-generated photo of her posing on a bed and says her political opponents have been using such images to try to undermine her.

items.title: Arsenal reach Champions League final by beating Atletico Madrid to close in on historic double
items.summary: Arsenal reach the Champions League final for the second time in their history with a 1-0 home win over Atletico Madrid sealing a 2-1 aggregate victory in the semi-final.

items.title: Project Freedom has been paused, so will it lead to a diplomatic breakthrough or reignite war with Iran?
items.summary: Iran had branded Project Freedom as "Project Deadlock" and now US president Donald Trump has placed the military's support mission in the Strait of Hormuz on hold.

items.title: Talia Gibson recovers from dreadful second set to beat Martina Trevisan in Rome
items.summary: Talia Gibson has made a fighting start on her debut on the clay of Rome, the Australian number two beating former world top-20 player Martina Trevisan to progress.

items.title: How do negative gearing, capital gains tax and trusts work?

items.title: Families could save up to $50 a week growing their own fruit and vegetables

items.title: EV tax discount removed two days' worth of carbon emissions

items.title: Trump warns Iran of 'much higher level' attacks if peace deal not agreed

items.title: Charges dismissed against Salamanca Whisky Bar for allegedly selling 'fake' Sullivans Cove whisky

items.title: Business as usual for Victoria's budget as inflation and interest rise before the state election

items.title: Businesses need to raise prices or put Australians out of work

items.title: 'Australia's first attractive comedian' Elouise Eftos looks for love in Aphrodite
items.summary: Last year, Elouise Eftos became the first Australian woman to land a best newcomer nod at Edinburgh Fringe. Now, she's got a new show about never falling in love.

items.title: Embracing being 'wasian' with a little help from Laufey
items.summary: Being biracial or "wasian" (that is, both white and Asian) is a unique experience. There are lots of things I share with both Asian and white people, but there are experiences novel to being wasian.
